title: Understanding the Problem
text: The woodpecker's head comes to a stop from an initial velocity of $$0.600$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$ over a distance of $$2.00$$ mm. We need to find the time it takes to stop.

title: Identify the Known Values
text: We know the initial velocity $$v_i$$ is $$0.600$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$ and the stopping distance (d) is $$2.00$$ mm. Convert the distance to meters for consistency in units.

title: Convert Units
text: Convert $$2.00$$ mm to meters. Remember, $$1$$ mm $$=$$ $$0.001$$ $$m$$.

title: Calculate the Stopping Distance in Meters
text: What is the stopping distance in meters?

title: Use the Kinematic Equation
text: Use the kinematic equation $${v_f}^2={v_i}^2+2a d$$ to find the acceleration (a), where $$v_f$$ is the final velocity (0 m/s).

title: Calculate the Acceleration
text: What is the acceleration of the woodpecker's head?

title: Find the Stopping Time
text: Use the equation $$v_f=v_i+a t$$ to find the time (t) it takes to stop.

title: Calculate the Stopping Time
text: What is the stopping time in seconds?
